case.title: NEW ORLEANS INSURANCE EXCHANGE v. UNITED STATES

opinion.h: NEW ORLEANS INSURANCE EXCHANGE v. UNITED STATES, 355 U.S. 22 (1957)
NEW ORLEANS INSURANCE EXCHANGE v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF LOUISIANA. No. 399. Decided October 28, 1957.
opinion.p: Murray F. Cleveland and Byron R. Kantrow for appellant.
Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Hansen and Charles H. Weston for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
355 U.S. 22 HURT v. OKLAHOMA. APPEAL FROM THE CRIMINAL COURT OF APPEALS OF OKLAHOMA. No. 402. Decided October 28, 1957.
Herbert K. Hyde and Sid White for appellant.
Mac Q. Williamson, Attorney General, and Sam H. Lattimore, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
